mainEntity.name: How much does a 306DT (3.0 TDV6/SDV6) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 306DT TDV6/SDV6 typically costs £6,500–£9,500 supply only, varying by power output and specification. A used unit can be found from £3,500–£5,500, but history is absolutely critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the 306DT (3.0 TDV6/SDV6) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 306DT has a mixed reputation (3.0★). While many examples exceed 150,000 miles, the well-documented crankshaft bearing failure can strike without warning, particularly on poorly maintained examples. Scrupulous oil change intervals are essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common 306DT failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crankshaft bearing failure is the most catastrophic — the tin-aluminium bearing coating can fatigue, causing sudden failure and bottom-end destruction. Cold-start knock is the early warning sign. Other common issues include turbo contamination and oil pump problems.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the 306DT?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 306DT powers the Discovery 4 and 5, Range Rover Sport, Range Rover, and Defender — spanning 211–306 hp outputs across TDV6, SDV6, D250, D300, and D350 variants.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover 306DT the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 306DT is shared with Jaguar fitments in the XF, XJ, and F-Pace. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a 306DT typically takes 15–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies by model and engine bay access.
